text: The surgeon performs a resection of the paracervix, bladder, and rectal pillar at the level of the cervix, aiming to remove the cervix in two parts. The procedure involves unroofing the ureter and maintaining a distance of at least ten millimeters from the uterus and vagina. The main goal is to preserve the neuronal part of the paracervix while conducting a paracervical lymphadenectomy.

The surgeon performs dissection of the paravesical space, dividing the uterine artery into paravesical in front and pararectal in the back. The pararectal space is further divided into medial (Okabashi space) and lateral (Latsuko space) sections. The ureter is visualized reaching the promontory level, guiding the dissection through avascular pelvic spaces.

The surgeon uses a bipolar device in one hand and a harmonic ultrasonic device in the other to perform the transection of the uterine pedicle. The advanced bipolar device, similar to a ligature, is employed for precise tissue dissection.
